pattern: Because a palindrome reads the same from both ends, you grow the path symmetrically from its center, tracking the two frontier nodes and the visited set as a bitmask.
hints: A palindromic path is determined by its center; build outward so that each newly added pair of nodes carries matching labels.
Keep states of the form (left frontier node, right frontier node, visited bitmask) where the two frontier labels already match, and the implied path between them is a palindrome.

Seed states from single nodes (odd center) and from label-matching edges (even center), then repeatedly extend each frontier to an unvisited neighbor so the two new nodes share a label, recording the longest length reached.

edge_inputs.description: star where a branch detour yields bcb
all same label, longest simple path wins
cycle where the palindrome must avoid revisiting
even-length palindrome centered on an edge

stress.description: small connected graph with a tiny alphabet so palindromic paths appear; size kept low for the exponential oracle
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 7))
    nodes = list(range(n))
    rng.shuffle(nodes)
    edges = []
    present = set()
    for i in range(1, n):
        u = nodes[i]
        v = nodes[rng.randint(0, i - 1)]
        a, b = (u, v) if u < v else (v, u)
        present.add((a, b))
    extra = rng.randint(0, n)
    for _ in range(extra):
        a = rng.randint(0, n - 1)
        b = rng.randint(0, n - 1)
        if a == b:
            continue
        if a > b:
            a, b = b, a
        present.add((a, b))
    edges = [[a, b] for (a, b) in present]
    label = ''.join(rng.choice('ab') for _ in range(n))
    return [n, edges, label]
